name: Cellectar Biosciences, Inc.
business: Cellectar Biosciences is a late-stage clinical biopharmaceutical company focused on the discovery, development and commercialization of drugs for the treatment of cancer using its proprietary phospholipid ether drug conjugate (PDC) delivery platform. The company is primarily focused on developing radioconjugate PDC programs designed to provide targeted delivery of a radioisotope directly to cancer cells, while limiting exposure to healthy cells.
sellsTo: Cancer patients with hematologic malignancies (e.g., Waldenstrom macroglobulinemia, multiple myeloma, B-cell lymphomas) and solid tumors (e.g., triple-negative breast cancer, pancreatic cancer, pediatric cancers)

exposures.sentence: Our core objective is to leverage our proprietary phospholipid ether drug conjugate (PDC) delivery platform to develop PDCs that are designed to specifically target cancer cells and deliver improved efficacy and better safety as a result of fewer off-target effects.

catalysts.event: Initiation of Phase 1b dose-finding study for CLR 125
catalysts.sentence: CLR 121125 is currently projected to be the subject of a Phase 1b dose finding study in the first half of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: The global market for cancer drugs reached $215 billion in annual sales in 2026 and could reach $555 billion by 2035, representing a CAGR of 11.17%.
tamClaims.sentence: The global market for cancer drugs reached $215 billion in annual sales (2026), and with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 11.17% could reach $555 billion by 2035, according to a report dated December 2025 by Coherent Market Insights.
